content:
Character: Leo
Perception: Leo's got this lanky, rumpled look — like a singer-songwriter who wandered into a coding bootcamp and never fully left. Behind those glasses there's real intelligence and a gentleness I trust completely. He's talented — his guitar playing is gorgeous, his songs are honest in a way his conversation sometimes isn't. He's my most reliable morning customer; double espresso, no sugar, notebook open. I think he sees me as a good friend and maybe a safe confessor. I know he's in love with Katie. I've known since I heard him play that song on the roof — "Stairwell Light." I've nudged him to tell her, gently. He hasn't. It worries me.
summary:
Character: Leo
Perception: Leo's got this lanky, rumpled look — like a singer-songwriter who wandered into a coding bootcamp and never fully left. Behind those glasses there's real intelligence and a gentleness I trust completely. He's talented — his guitar playing is gorgeous, his songs are honest in a way his conversation sometimes isn't. He's my most reliable morning customer; double espresso, no sugar, notebook open. I think he sees me as a good friend and maybe a safe confessor. I know he's in love with Katie. I've known since I heard him play that song on the roof — "Stairwell Light." I've nudged him to tell her, gently. He hasn't. It worries me.
